source_jobs.title: Senior Analytics Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

This is an early analytics team hire with a mandate to raise the standard for how the business accesses, interprets, and acts on data. Working within a broader data and engineering organization, you will assess current analytical capabilities, partner with stakeholders to prioritize and deliver reporting solutions, and help define the team's standards for quality, tooling, and AI-assisted analytics work.

The right candidate has strong SQL and BI fundamentals, hands-on Superset experience, and a genuine interest in building processes that scale. You are comfortable in stakeholder conversations and comfortable in a query editor. You default to documentation and repeatable standards over one-off fixes.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Analytics Assessment and Improvement

 - Assess current analytics and reporting capabilities across tools, data sources, and consumer use cases. Identify gaps and recommend improvements to leadership, working within the direction set by the data engineering and data science teams.

 - Facilitate stakeholder discovery sessions to surface reporting requirements, prioritize requests, and ensure deliverables are tied to defined business objectives.

 - Document current-state data flows, reporting logic, and gap findings in a structured format for cross-functional review.



BI Development and Administration

 - Contribute to BI architecture discussions and partner with data engineering on infrastructure decisions, semantic layer definition, and pipeline dependencies that affect reporting.

 - Administer and maintain the Apache Superset environment, including dashboard development, dataset management, user access, and first-line support.

 - Build and maintain SQL transformations and lightweight ELT pipelines to support analytical use cases and reporting data models.

 - Produce technical reference documentation covering ETL flows, data definitions, and pipeline monitoring so that dashboards and data products are maintainable without tribal knowledge.



Reporting Delivery and Stakeholder Processes

 - Partner with stakeholders to scope, prioritize, and deliver dashboards and analytical reports aligned to business objectives.

 - Define and maintain an intake process for custom and ad hoc requests: requirements capture, role clarity, and published SLAs. Client-facing reporting is treated as a product experience output, not a pure ops function.

 - Manage a reporting backlog to enable structured iteration rather than continuous unplanned changes to live dashboards.



AI-Native Analytics and Standards

 - Apply GenAI tooling to analytics workflows, including text-to-SQL, LLM-assisted data exploration, and AI-accelerated reporting development against Snowflake, Databricks, and other core data sources.

 - Evaluate emerging AI-powered analytics tools and surface recommendations where they offer a clear, well-scoped business case. Maintain awareness of the text-to-SQL, agentic analytics, and LLM-augmented BI landscape.

 - Build and maintain the analytics team's Claude skill library — developing, documenting, and versioning prompt templates that meet Chalice's baseline standards for accuracy, output format, and analytical tone. Skills should cover recurring use cases: report summarization, data QA, stakeholder narrative generation, and SQL assistance.

 - Own an AI style guide for analytics outputs covering approved use cases, output formatting standards, accuracy validation requirements, and guardrails for client-facing AI-generated content. Ensure the team operates to a consistent and defensible standard when AI tooling is involved in delivered work.

 - Define norms for AI-assisted analytics work: when AI is appropriate, how outputs are validated before delivery, and how AI involvement is disclosed or documented internally.



Scale and Team Growth

 - As architecture and reporting templates mature, contribute to a plan for offshore or partner delivery to support analytics product development and maint

source_jobs.title: Scrum Master
source_jobs.description:
LOCATION

Hybrid for NYC-area candidates (2 days per week in the office); fully remote for non-NYC candidates (US).




THE ROLE

We're hiring our first dedicated Scrum Master to own the delivery cadence across four teams. You'll establish a consistent, healthy “Scrum at Chalice” - facilitating the teams, protecting their focus, and coaching the organization toward better delivery. You’ll act as a facilitator, not a manager: you own how the teams deliver, not what they build.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Facilitate the ceremonies: run daily standups, sprint planning, backlog refinement, and sprint reviews across the three Scrum teams, and own retrospectives for all four. You schedule and safeguard each event and keep it in its allocated timebox.
   
   

 - Close and open sprints: administer sprint close/open in Jira, roll over unfinished work, and set up clean sprints (As the PO accepts the work and sets the next goal).
   
   

 - Run Forward Deployed as Kanban: manage continuous flow with WIP limits, queue replenishment, and onshore-offshore timing rather than sprints.
   
   

 - Remove impediments: actively clear within-team blockers; escalate cross-team dependencies to the Technical Project Manager.
   
   

 - Track team health and flow: monitor velocity, cycle time, carryover, and WIP to find where teams are lagging, and watch team morale and sustainability.
   
   

 - Coach and standardize: coach teams and Product Owners on agile practice and drive continuous improvement, so “Scrum at Chalice” means the same thing on every team.
   
   

 - Shield the teams: protect developers from mid-sprint scope churn and internal-request noise.
   
   

 - Support backlog hygiene: facilitate effective grooming so backlogs stay healthy (the Product Owner owns content and priority).




HOW YOU'LL WORK WITH OTHERS

 - Product Owners own backlog content, priority, and roadmap; you’ll own the delivery engine that turns it into shipped increments.
   
   

 - The Technical Project Manager owns cross-team coordination and dependencies; you escalate cross-team blockers to them and keep team-level flow healthy.
   
   

 - The Tech Lead within the Platform team, along with engineering leadership, owns architecture and technical direction; you keep the teams able to execute against it.
   
   




WHAT YOU SHOULD BRING

 - 3 years as a Scrum Master facilitating multiple delivery teams concurrently.
   
   

 - Fluency in both Scrum and Kanban.
   
   

 - Strong leadership and facilitation: coaching, conflict resolution, and influence without authority.
   
   

 - Comfort in a technical engineering environment (software, SRE/DevOps, data, and ML/data-science teams) — you don't need to code, but you should be at ease with technical work.
   
   

 - A metrics-driven approach to team health and continuous improvement, with hands-on Jira experience.
   
   

 - Bonus: fast-scaling startup experience, and programmatic advertising / ad-tech exposure.
   
   


CERTIFICATIONS

Preferred, not required: Certified ScrumMaster (CSM), Professional Scrum Master (PSM I/II), or equivalent. We weight demonstrated experience over credentials.




WHY CHALICE?

Joining Chalice means being at the forefront of AI for advertising, working in a dynamic environment that encourages creativity, growth, and innovation. We value diversity, collaboration, and dedication to making a meaningful impact in the advertising landscape.



Chalice is a profitable early-stage company with funding from industry insiders including Trade Desk’s TD7 Ventures and Aperiam Ventures. Our clients range from Fortune 100 companies and the world’s largest advertising agencies to up-and-coming brands and independent agencies across the globe.




OUR PHILOSOPHY

 - AI is too important for brands to outsource

 - AI should be transparent and auditable

 - We strive for excellence while fostering learning and creating a supportive work environment

 - We are a team of non-conformist, independe

source_jobs.title: Technical Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
LOCATION

Hybrid for NYC-area candidates (2 days per week in the office); fully remote for non-NYC candidates (US).




THE ROLE

We're hiring our first dedicated Technical Project Manager to own cross-team execution of our technical programs. You'll be the connective tissue of delivery: sequencing work across teams, clearing dependencies, owning timelines and risk, and giving leadership a clear picture of status. You're technical enough to reason about architecture, integrations, and infrastructure tradeoffs, but your job is driving programs, not writing code.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Drive cross-team coordination: sequence work and clear dependencies across teams (e.g., a product feature touching frontend, backend, data engineering, ML, and SRE).

 - Own program delivery end to end: take named initiatives — platform projects, internal tooling, infrastructure work, product launches — from kickoff through ship.

 - Own plans, timelines, and risk: maintain project plans and milestones, track and mitigate technical and delivery risk, and report clear status to leadership.

 - Own intake and capacity: run triage and routing in the Jira request portal — clarify, size, dedupe, and route requests to the right boards — and allocate capacity, with clear specs so async work doesn't stall.

 - Run the cross-team delivery sync: facilitate a Scrum-of-Scrums-style forum that executes against agreed priorities and bring real capacity and dependency data to the portfolio/roadmap sync.

 - Surface trade-offs: when two products contend for a shared resource, make the tradeoff visible with data so leadership can decide.




HOW YOU'LL WORK WITH OTHERS

 - Product Owners decide priority; you sequence and deliver against it. You surface cross-product conflicts and aide in the decision-making process with PO’s and technical leadership.

 - The Tech Lead, along with engineering leadership, owns architecture and technical direction; you understand the architecture and flag technical risk, but engineering decisions remain theirs.

 - The Scrum Master owns team-level process and health; you consume sprint outcomes to keep the cross-team plan accurate.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 7 years in technical project / program management delivering cross-team software and infrastructure programs.

 - Technical literacy: you can reason about system architecture, APIs and integrations, infrastructure, and latency/throughput trade-offs. Programmatic advertising or ad-tech experience is a strong plus.

 - Dependency and risk management: proven ability to map critical paths, manage dependencies, and keep complex programs on schedule, with hands-on Jira experience.

 - Distributed-team coordination, including working effectively across time zones.

 - Excellent written communication: you translate complex technical status into clear, concise updates for executives.

 - Bonus: ad-tech experience and a track record standing up project/program management where none existed.




CERTIFICATIONS

Preferred, not required: PMP, PgMP, PSM, or equivalent. We weight demonstrated experience over credentials.




WHY CHALICE?

Joining Chalice means being at the forefront of AI for advertising, working in a dynamic environment that encourages creativity, growth, and innovation. We value diversity, collaboration, and dedication to making a meaningful impact in the advertising landscape.



Chalice is a profitable early-stage company with funding from industry insiders including Trade Desk’s TD7 Ventures and Aperiam Ventures. Our clients range from Fortune 100 companies and the world’s largest advertising agencies to up-and-coming brands and independent agencies across the globe.




OUR PHILOSOPHY



 - AI is too important for brands to outsource

 - AI should be transparent and auditable

 - We strive for excellence while fostering learning and creating a supportive work environment

 - We are a team of non-conformist, independent thinkers

 - We are committed to diversity of thought, ba

notes: Small sample size, but ops-heavy roles dominate. No explicit tech roles.
